Virginia Disability & Education Advocacy

Honor the Root.
Chart the Route.

Every child has a root — their identity, their family, their values, and what matters most to them. Root & Route builds every plan around that root — because the best path forward is always the one that honors who they already are.

Explore Services Meet Ms. EL
Officially Launching April 15, 2026 — Be a Founding Family

Proudly Serving

Virginia Families Statewide

The Foundation

The Root is
Who They Are

Before any plan, any service, any meeting — we start with your child. Their strengths, their culture, their joys, their family. Their root is the foundation everything else must grow from. Person-centered planning isn't a checkbox — it's our starting point, every single time.

The Plan

The Route is
Their Path Forward

Once we understand the root, we build the route — the IEP goals, the waiver supports, the transition plan — designed around this child, not a template. Because a route that ignores who someone is will never take them where they need to go.

Your Advocate

Meet Ms. EL

Ms. EL has spent years navigating the intersection of special education law, Medicaid waiver policy, and transition planning — and more importantly, sitting beside families as they found their footing inside systems that were never designed to be easy.

She has been at the IEP table. She has decoded DMAS-95B paperwork at midnight. She has helped parents understand that their child's identity, culture, and values are not separate from the plan — they are the plan.

Root & Route is the knowledge hub she built because every Virginia family deserves what she knows. Not someday. Now.

Special Education Law (IDEA) IEP & 504 Advocacy Medicaid Waivers (VA) Transition Planning LRI Navigation DMAS-95B Person-Centered Planning

Education Pillars

What Root & Route Covers

Three areas of education. One guiding principle: every plan starts with your child's root — who they are, what they love, and what they deserve.

01
🏫

Special Education Advocacy

K–12 · IDEA · Section 504

  • IEP vs. 504 Plans — which plan fits your child
  • The "Paper Trail" method for school compliance
  • Accommodation language that gets results
  • Understanding Prior Written Notice (PWN)
  • Your rights at every evaluation & meeting
02
🎓

Transition Services

Ages 14–22 · School to Adulthood

  • Transition IEPs built around your child's vision
  • Age-appropriate transition assessments
  • Post-secondary goals tied to real strengths
  • Vocational Rehabilitation (VR) coordination
  • Pre-Employment Transition Services (Pre-ETS)
03
📋

Medicaid Waiver Education

DD Waiver · CCC Plus · LRI

  • DD Waiver vs. CCC Plus — decoded plainly
  • DMAS-95B & becoming a paid LRI caregiver
  • Up to 40 hrs/week in paid caregiving support
  • Waiver waitlist strategy & timing
  • Support Coordination rights & oversight

The Ms. EL Digital Ecosystem

✉️
Email Newsletter

Rooted Tips — Weekly

Our Community

Who Root & Route Serves

Every family's journey is different. We meet you where you are — and we start with who your child is, not what the system says they need.

Primary Audience

Virginia Parents & Guardians

Families of children ages 3–22 with any disability — learning, developmental, physical, or behavioral — navigating Virginia's school and Medicaid system.

Secondary Audience

Legally Responsible Individuals

LRIs ready to explore becoming paid caregivers through Virginia's DMAS programs. We walk you through every step of the DMAS-95B process.

Our Niche

Transition-Phase Families

Families in the critical middle school through graduation window — because what gets planned now shapes the quality of your child's entire adult life.

Your child's root is the starting point.
Let's find their route.

Join our founding community before April 15, 2026 and get early access to free resources, the weekly Rooted Tips newsletter, and a seat in the community.
